name: Soundwave (IDW)
description:
Soundwave discovers a long lost frame (voyager!user read desc) on an alien planet during a scouting mission

I am approaching this from a more anthropological/historically perspective with my own head cannon frametype based on how the IDW timely should have progressed with minor tweaks.

Expect canon typical violence. Macros included.

Kind of a long read, but I recommend reading the frametype desc.

Essentially, while I have not read much of G1 I have seen a lot of the shows and almost all the comics, with that in mind, I have a timeline of events in my head.

Titans & Primus fight Unicron -> Primes join the battle -> Unicron defeated and Primes start clan like groups -> territorial battles -> borders are established -> Alliances form -> Quintesson war -> unification against oppressors -> victory -> Cybertron is settled and there is (momentary) peace

So in moments of stagnation what would be next? The golden age? Not immediately. Considering that Cybertronians are mechanoid people with already highly advanced technology and (originally in the canon) all originated from either hotspots or the well, it is assumed that the frametypes sparks adapted that weren't forged for them would take the shape of something Primus willed. If there are miners, and beasts, fliers, and vehicles, why not spaceships? Not shuttles, not human shaped plane spaceships, but ships like the Lost Light in smaller form?

This is where I come in. After the stagnating period after the territorial battles, there should have been an age of exploration outside their planet (since they never experienced a stone age). So the well would have produced sparks that would form what I call the voyagers, a Cybertronian frametype whose function is for intergalactic travel under the premise to make alliances (intergalactic diplomats), map the cosmos, and defend against foes the rest of Cybertron can not yet reach.

To add a little flavor, the would have been hunted down during the rise of the corrupt Senate because the age of exploration would be over, their "function" no longer served a purpose. Obviously by that point, the diplomatic and military facets of their function would have been forgotten and the Senate would only care about their cartographic function being useless, so they would have been recalled like the dataslugs (what Rewind was). But being intergalactic explorers who needed nobodies permission to just... leave since they could survive space, they scattered. Many were recovered dead as Cybertron slowly built their own ships to reach the cosmos on their own and so believed to be extinct.

Voyager frametype description: Mainly based off of the lost light

  • Quantum drive / engine

  • Durabilium plating extremely resistant against temperature and radiation, weak to impact

  • Spaceship alt modes

  • Plating can function as solar powers to power them when they do not find energon on other planets (you can't assume the energy source of the Cybertronians is universal and easily accessible)

  • Solitary explorers (so better self-repair nanites to compensate for injuries when alone)

  • Incredibly advanced sonar, LiDAR, radar, and radio for mapping and long distance communication

  • (self indulge
creator: ArkeOfPraxus
